sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 8.14 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.8)

statement.latex: b) Assume a group$G$is existentially closed in one of the classes$L{ N}^{+}$, \$L{ S}_{ }$, \$L{ S}^{+}$, \$L{ S}$of, respectively, all locally nilpotent torsion-free groups, all locally soluble$$-groups, all locally soluble torsion-free groups, or all locally soluble groups. Is it true that$G$is characteristically simple? The existential closedness of$G$is a local property, thus it seems difficul...
